title: Battery minerals for neural inhibition
abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02620-8 Triphylite (LiFePO4), a lithium mineral battery cathode, is incorporated into a bioelectronic device for controlled and localized lithium delivery to neurons, establishing a precision strategy for neural inhibition.

title: High impact resistance in a high-entropy alloy with thermally stable hierachical heterostructures
abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02626-2 Face-centred cubic high-entropy alloys with a core–shell heterostructure, uniformly distributed nanoscale precipitates and oxide nanoparticles in the shell are developed. title: Tracking microstructure and mechanical response
abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 03 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02633-3 Characterizing structural evolution alongside mechanical behaviour is challenging for micro- and nanostructured materials, highlighting the need for advancing in situ characterization techniques.

title: In situ mechanical characterization of functional and architected materials
abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 03 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02601-x Recent developments in in situ micro- and nanoscale mechanical characterization, equipped with exceptional spatial and temporal resolution, are expanding frontiers in the study of functional materials, whose properties…

title: Boosting ionic conductivity of single-ion conductive polyelectrolyte elastomers via high-dielectric plasticizers
abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 03 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02604-8 Polyelectrolyte elastomers support selective and leakage-free ionic transport, but suffer from low ionic conductivity. A solid-state dielectric additive is introduced to various polyelectrolyte elastomers, which increas…

title: Magnetoelectric microrobots for spinal cord injury regeneration
abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 02 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02625-3 Microrobots made of human-induced pluripotent stem cells and magnetoelectric nanoparticles promote lesion repair and motor improvements after spinal cord injury in zebrafish and mice under magnetic guidance and stimulat…

title: Van der Waals strain hardening and large uniform tensile elongation in GaSe
abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 02 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02614-6 Substantial strain hardening can be triggered in GaSe and other similar chalcogenide single crystals at particular loading directions inclined to the [0001] zone axis via alternating interlayer slip.
